_comment: WC2026 Round of 32 (Last 32), 2026-06-28, Los Angeles Stadium. KNOCKOUT: there is no draw -- a level game after 90' goes to extra time and then penalties, so the deliverable is who ADVANCES, not the 1X2. Group routes: Canada finished 2nd in Group B (1-1 Bosnia, 6-0 Qatar, 1-2 Switzerland; rating 75) -- attacking but the 6-0 vs Qatar inflates the goal record, and they lost to the one strong side (Switzerland). South Africa finished 2nd in Group A (0-2 Mexico, 1-1 Czech, 1-0 South Korea; rating 68) -- an organized, defensive grinder that wins 1-0 and scores late. IMPORTANT calibration memory: South Africa was my single worst group-stage miss (I downgraded them as 'virtually eliminated' and they beat South Korea 1-0 to qualify) -- so do NOT under-rate them; respect the low block. KNOCKOUT-SPECIFIC reads: (1) no rotation, full motivation both sides (win or go home). (2) South Africa's stubborn low block keeps the game tight and low-scoring (the England-Ghana/organized-underdog archetype) -> that RAISES the chance the tie reaches extra time/penalties, where it is closer to a coin-flip -> so South Africa's ADVANCEMENT probability is higher than its 90' win probability. Canada the favourite by class but must break the block down. Home = Canada (nominal; neutral venue). Cards not modelled (referee TBD).

meta.stakes: Single-elimination: level after 90' -> extra time -> penalties. Canada the favourite by class; South Africa a disciplined low block that grinds 1-0s -> the tight game raises the ET/penalty chance, lifting South Africa's advancement above its 90' win odds.

home.name: Canada
notes: Rating 75; attacking (8 goals in the group) but the 6-0 vs Qatar inflates it and they lost 1-2 to Switzerland, the one strong side faced. attack 1.18.
Decent but not airtight defensively. defense 0.95.
Favourite by class; must break down a packed block.

away.name: South Africa
notes: Rating 68; organized, low-scoring grinder (1-0 South Korea, 1-1 Czech, scores late). attack 0.90 -- NO over-downgrade (they were my worst group miss).
Disciplined, hard to break down. defense 0.92 -> keeps it tight and low-scoring.
Knockout: the tight game drags toward ET/penalties, where the underdog's advancement odds rise.

_postmatch.result: Canada 1-0 South Africa. Canada advance to the Round of 16. Settled in 90' -- no extra time needed.
_postmatch.model_1x2: Canada 44.7 / Draw 28.9 / South Africa 26.4 (LOW, 90'); advancement Canada 60.4 / South Africa 39.6
_postmatch.verdict: Clean process hit. The favourite advanced, and the TEXTURE was exactly the read: a tight, low-scoring grind (1-0 was a top-3 scoreline at 11.4%; Under 2.5 was 56%). xG Canada 1.40 -> 1 goal, near-exact; South Africa 1.02 -> 0, their low block kept it tight (BTTS No). Crucially I did NOT over-downgrade South Africa this time (they were my single worst group miss) -- I respected the block, which is why the model expected a narrow 1-0 rather than a rout, and that is what happened. It was settled in 90' (the model's 71% branch), so the ET/penalty tail did not need to fire.
_postmatch.lesson: The knockout-aware read held: respect a disciplined low block -> expect a tight, low-scoring tie that the favourite edges (or that drags to ET/penalties). The advancement framing (Canada 60.4%) correctly sat above the 90' win (44.7%) -- and here the favourite simply closed it out in regulation. The South-Africa lesson (don't fade the grinder) was applied and validated.
